title: Spicy Gooey Egyptian Mussels Toast
description: A spicy and savory Egyptian-inspired dish featuring mussels, garlic, and spices, served with crispy bread for dipping.

ingredients.name: Egyptian dukkah (or substitute with a mix of spices)
White wine (or substitute with lemon juice)

instructions: In a small bowl, mix together dukkah, garlic, cayenne pepper, and salt.
Rinse the mussels under cold water and remove any broken shells.
Heat the avocado oil in a large cast-iron skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the mussels to the skillet in a single layer, leaving some space between each mussel.
Pour the white wine (or lemon juice) over the mussels and cover the skillet with a lid.
Cook for 3-4 minutes, or until the mussels start to open.
Sprinkle the dukkah mixture evenly over the mussels and continue cooking for another 2-3 minutes.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the mussels are cooked through.
Meanwhile, toast the bread slices until crispy.
Serve the mussels hot with the toasted bread for dipping and garnish with parsley and cilantro. Serve with lemon wedges on the side.
Drizzle with extra avocado oil, if desired.
